summary: Smoking Help‑Slip‑Franklin run & soaring Morning Dew
blurb: The second set delivers a blistering Help On The Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower sequence that flows into an expansive Space → Morning Dew jam. Brent’s organ roars, Jerry’s leads are on fire, and the soundboard capture is crystal‑clear throughout.
review: June 27, 1984 at Merriweather Post Pavilion captures the Dead in top form. The first set rolls through solid rockers – Jack Straw, The Loser, Let It Grow – but it’s the second set that steals the show. A smoking Help On The Way → Slipknot! → Franklin's Tower medley launches into a tightly‑locked Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World → Why Don't We Do It In The Road segue before the classic drums‑Space‑Morning Dew stretch, culminating in an ecstatic Throwing Stones and a rousing Not Fade Away. Brent Mydland’s Hammond B‑3 and piano drive the jams, especially on Morning Dew where his soulful lines match Keith’s ’77 vibe. Jerry Garcia’s guitar is soaring and warm; even when his voice shows a hint of rasp on Eyes Of The World it never dulls the overall brilliance. Phil Lesh lays down booming, “whale‑like” bass bombs that anchor the improvisations, while Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann keep a tight, propulsive rhythm foundation. The soundboard source is consistently praised as crisp, deep and virtually hiss‑free, making this one of the cleanest 1984 recordings available – perfect for fans who want to hear the band at full throttle.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All nine recordings (except one mixed) carry a positive sentiment, repeatedly praising the Help‑Slip‑Franklin sequence, Morning Dew jam, Brent’s keyboards and overall band tightness. No consistent negative comments about performance appear; only isolated notes of vocal rasp or minor tempo rushes. The strong, unanimous acclaim warrants a high positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crisp, deep soundboard capture with excellent clarity; widely praised as one of the best SBDs for this show.

key_highlights: Smoking Help On The Way → Slipknot! → Franklin's Tower sequence that launches the second‑set energy.
Soaring Morning Dew jam emerging from a seamless Space segment.
Brent Mydland’s organ and piano work drive the jams, drawing comparisons to Keith’s 1977 playing.
Crystal‑clear soundboard recording that preserves every nuance of the high‑energy performance.

band_performance.Jerry: Guitar work is stellar – soaring leads, warm clean tone and powerful riffs; vocals show a slight rasp on Eyes of the World but remain strong overall.
band_performance.Phil: Bass is booming and mind‑boggling, delivering deep “whale” bombs that lock in with the band; also praised for fun vocal moments on Do It In The Road.
band_performance.Bob: Vocals described as laid‑back yet powerful, putting him in peak front‑man form during the set.
band_performance.Brent: Organ/B3 roars and piano lines are a highlight, providing soulful drive to Morning Dew and the Help‑Slip‑Franklin sequence; likened to Keith’s 1977 brilliance.
band_performance.Mickey: Drumming tight, powerful and drives the second‑set jams with precision.
band_performance.Billy: Locks in tightly with Mickey, delivering driving rhythms that underpin the improvisations.

must_listen_sequences: Why Don't We Do It In The Road*
